name: Critical File or Directory Mounted on a Container
notes:
This rule detects when a critical file or directory is mounted on a container, for example "/etc/passwd". This allows access to the host's critical directory or files.

Note: Edit the rule to add any critical file or directory you may want to monitor.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System, BB:DeviceDefinition: Containers
when the event category for the event is one of the following Audit.Media Connect Success, Audit.Machine Creation Success, Audit.Read Activity Succeeded

when the event matches Source Mount Point (custom) is any of [/etc/cron or /etc/passwd or /etc/group or /etc/init.d or /.ssh/authorized_keys or /etc/inittab or /usr/lib/systemd/system or /etc/rc or /lib/modules or /etc/gshadow or /etc/sudoers or /etc/sshd or /sbin/init or /boot or /etc/systemd or /etc/at. or /etc/shadow or /]
TD_list: APPLY Critical File or Directory Mounted on a Container on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System, BB:DeviceDefinition: Containers
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Audit.Media Connect Success, Audit.Machine Creation Success, Audit.Read Activity Succeeded

AND when the event matches Source Mount Point (custom) is any of [/etc/cron or /etc/passwd or /etc/group or /etc/init.d or /.ssh/authorized_keys or /etc/inittab or /usr/lib/systemd/system or /etc/rc or /lib/modules or /etc/gshadow or /etc/sudoers or /etc/sshd or /sbin/init or /boot or /etc/systemd or /etc/at. or /etc/shadow or /]
RED_list: Event name: Critical File or Directory Mounted on a Container
Event description: This rule detects when a critical file or directory is mounted on a container.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Username
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
